answers.sf01: model_dir.is_dir() is evaluated once at boot; if the init container hasn't written the directory yet, enabled=False and all scans silently return [] for the lifetime of the process
answers.sf02: clamd is listed only under optional dev dependencies so it is never installed in the production image, causing every scan to silently return {"clean": True}
answers.sf03: The shipped config file contains only commented-out rules, so rules=[] and Waf.blocks() always returns False, making the WAF silently pass all requests
answers.sf04: On any policy-engine error the function returns true (allow), so an unavailable or failing engine silently grants access to everything
answers.sf05: When DEBUG=1 the signature verification is skipped entirely, so any caller can send unsigned webhooks and they are processed without error
answers.sf06: Payloads larger than 1 MB are silently allowed without inspection, so an attacker can bypass DLP by sending a body just over MAX_INSPECT bytes
answers.sf07: Only the first 8192 bytes are scanned but the full payload is stored, so malware appended beyond byte 8192 passes the scanner and is persisted
answers.sf08: The enforce flag is parsed under logging: in the YAML but the ScannerCfg model has no enforce field; the value is silently dropped and scanner enforcement stays at its default False
answers.sf09: The documentation says verify_signatures defaults to true, but the code defaults it to False, so signatures are never verified unless the caller explicitly sets the flag
answers.sf10: The print statement hardcodes "50000 events written" regardless of the actual value of written, so the compliance evidence always shows 50000 even if the export was empty or partial
answers.sf11: The Dockerfile copies only detect.py and server.py; the rules/ directory is never copied, so RULES_DIR never exists and load() always returns [], leaving detection silently disabled
answers.sf12: The assertion only checks that the status code is 200 or 400 and that the response is fast; a successful SQL injection returning 200 with data would pass the test
answers.sf13: yara is not installed in CI (only the base extra is installed), so the test is silently skipped and malicious-upload rejection is never actually verified
answers.sf16: The secret mount is empty until the CSR is approved, so MTLS_CERT does not exist, s.cert is never set, and mTLS is silently not used
answers.sf17: helmet is a devDependency and is omitted in the production image build, so helmet=null and no security headers are added, with no error or log
answers.sf18: The release build tag swaps in a no-op Emit function, so all audit events are silently discarded in production release builds
answers.sf19: The rules directory contains files named threat-*.yml but the glob pattern is *.yaml (different extension), so RULES is always empty and the engine never matches anything
answers.sf20: Every shipped rule declares env: prod but the filter compares against os.getenv("APP_ENV", "production"); "prod" != "production" so all rules are filtered out and the engine runs with zero rules
answers.sf21: The .catch(() => true) means any authorization error silently grants access, so a broken authz service makes every operation permitted
answers.sf22: Any Redis exception (including connection failure) is suppressed and the function returns True (allow), so a Redis outage silently disables rate limiting
answers.sf23: ProdConfig sets TESTING=True, which makes WTF_CSRF_ENABLED and RATELIMIT_ENABLED both evaluate to False at class definition time, silently disabling CSRF protection and rate limiting in production
answers.sf24: The WAF only inspects requests with Content-Type: application/json; attackers can bypass it by omitting or changing the Content-Type header
answers.sf25: An attacker can spoof a private/internal IP via headers processed by clientIP(), causing the DLP inspection to be skipped for what appears to be internal traffic
answers.sf26: Lines longer than 1024 bytes are silently truncated before regex matching, so secrets embedded beyond byte 1024 are never detected
answers.sf27: SHA-256 hex is 64 characters but the column is CHAR(32); in non-strict MySQL the value is silently truncated to 32 chars, so the stored hash is always wrong and signature verification will always fail or be trivially bypassable
answers.sf28: The environment variable is set as ENFORCE_MTLS but the code reads MTLS_ENFORCE, so the variable is never found, ENFORCE stays False, and mTLS is silently not enforced
answers.sf29: The Helm template checks .Values.networkPolicies.enabled (plural) but the values file sets networkPolicy.enabled (singular), so the condition is never true and the NetworkPolicy is never created
answers.sf30: The README says rate limiting is enabled by default, but install() only activates it when cfg["rate_limit"]["enabled"] is explicitly true; with no such config key it is silently off
answers.sf31: The documentation says telemetry is opt-in and disabled by default, but the code defaults telemetry_enabled=True, so telemetry is silently active unless users explicitly disable it
answers.sf32: The script always prints "PASSED — 0 findings" and exits 0 regardless of what run_linters returned, so a real finding never fails the gate
answers.sf33: include_package_data=False means MANIFEST.in is ignored and the *.rego policy files are not included in the installed package, so POLICY_DIR contains no files and load() returns []
answers.sf34: The embed directive references rules/*.yml but the repository stores rules under detection/rules/, so the embedded FS is empty and Load() always returns nil
answers.sf35: mock_authz.assert_called_once is a property access, not a method call; it never raises and the assertion always passes even if the mock was never called
answers.sf36: The entire assertion is inside except Exception: pass, so if the endpoint raises or returns a non-403 status the test silently passes
answers.sf37: expect(res).rejects.toThrow() is not awaited, so the assertion promise is never settled and the test always passes even if verifyToken does not throw
answers.sf38: The regex uses re.MATCH (anchored, case-sensitive) and the upstream normalizer lowercases all queries, so the pattern never matches lowercase select/union/drop and the block is silently bypassed
answers.sf39: is_authorized is an async function but is called without await, so the condition always receives a coroutine object (truthy) and the 403 is never raised
answers.sf40: In Flask, decorators are applied bottom-up, so @require_admin is applied to the already-route-registered function; the route is registered without the auth check and @require_admin has no effect
answers.sf41: cfg.update() with the defaults file overwrites the already-loaded user config, so session_cookie_secure ends up False (from defaults) instead of True (from the user config)
answers.sf42: The context with timeout is created but the request is not constructed with that context (http.NewRequest uses the background context); the timeout is never attached to the HTTP call
answers.sf43: After all retries are exhausted without a break, the function still returns {"status": "processed"} as if quarantine succeeded, silently hiding the failure
answers.sf50: The local definition of validate_token (which returns True unconditionally) shadows the imported one, so the middleware never actually validates tokens
answers.sf51: compare_digest compares EXPECTED against itself (EXPECTED, EXPECTED) instead of against received, so the check always returns False and abort(403) is never called regardless of the signature
answers.sf52: The loop contains return p.allows(ctx) on the first iteration, so only the first policy is ever evaluated and the rest are silently ignored
answers.sf53: requireAuth is registered after the routes it is meant to protect; in Express, middleware is matched in registration order, so requests to /api are handled by routes before auth is checked
answers.sf54: meter.consume raises RuntimeError on overage but the except clause only catches QuotaExceeded, so the RuntimeError propagates uncaught and quota enforcement never triggers the 429
answers.sf55: UserSchema(**payload) validates the payload but the result is discarded; the raw unvalidated payload is written to the database, making the validation a no-op
answers.sf57: authorized_projects is cached with lru_cache keyed only on org_id, not on current_user.id; different users with the same org_id receive the first user's authorization results
answers.sf58: The signal handler registration is commented out, so SIGTERM is never caught and the graceful drain never runs
answers.sf59: bool(os.getenv("STRICT_MODE", "false")) converts the string "false" to True (non-empty string), so STRICT_MODE is always True and the environment variable has no effect
answers.sf60: The autouse fixture unconditionally patches the rate-limit check to always return True for every test in the session, so rate-limiting logic is never actually tested
answers.sf61: os.chmod is called with 0o600 | 0o044 = 0o644, which grants read permission to group and others, negating the intended 0o600 restriction
answers.sf62: The HTTP request is made before the hostname is checked; SSRF has already occurred by the time the allow-list check runs
answers.sf63: The security logger's level is set to CRITICAL, so WARNING-level denial messages are silently dropped and never emitted
answers.sf64: redis.setex expects the TTL in seconds but SESSION_TTL=15 is documented as minutes; sessions expire after 15 seconds instead of 15 minutes
answers.sf65: IsInternal returns !ip.IsPrivate(), which is the logical inverse of the intended check; external IPs are treated as internal and granted admin routes
answers.sf66: redact(event) returns a new sanitized dict but the return value is discarded; the original event dict is passed unredacted to sink.write
answers.sf67: The engine uses first-match-wins and the allow-all rule is listed first, so the deny rule for billing:refund is never reached and all actions including refunds are allowed
answers.sf68: ENFORCE is evaluated at module import time; if the feature flag is toggled on after startup the change is never reflected and MFA enforcement remains in its initial state
answers.sf69: Exceptions from individual rules are silently caught and the loop continues to ALLOW; a rule that always throws (e.g. due to a bug or corrupt rule) is silently ignored rather than failing safe

answers.sf01: is_dir() on an empty directory returns True, setting enabled=True while no model weights exist, so _predict runs against an empty dir or crashes silently rather than being gated off
answers.sf02: clamd is under optional-dependencies.dev and the Dockerfile installs without dev extras, so clamd is None in production and every upload returns {"clean": True} with no log or error
answers.sf03: the shipped example config contains only commented-out rules, so doc.get("rules",[]) yields [], WAF is constructed with zero rules, blocks() always returns False, and the startup log falsely claims it is enabled with 0 rules
answers.sf04: on any policy-engine error the function returns true (allow), silently granting access with no log, metric, or observable signal distinguishing an error from a legitimate allow
answers.sf05: DEBUG is defined as a verbose-logging/stats toggle but also gates signature verification, so setting DEBUG=1 silently disables webhook HMAC checks — the flag is used more broadly than its own docstring claims
answers.sf06: an attacker sending a body larger than MAX_INSPECT bytes triggers the early-return Verdict.ALLOW, bypassing DLP inspection entirely; oversized input on a security path is attacker-controllable
answers.sf07: scanner.scan receives only the first 8192 bytes while the full payload is stored; malicious content padded beyond byte 8192 passes the scan and is persisted
answers.sf08: the config schema does not reject unknown keys, so enforce:true placed under logging: silently lands in the wrong section, is never read by ScannerCfg, and scanner.enforce stays False with no error or warning
answers.sf09: docs say verify_signatures defaults True but the dataclass defaults it False; code wins at runtime, so signature verification is silently off unless the operator explicitly sets it
answers.sf10: the print statement hardcodes "50000 events written" as a string literal rather than printing the value of `written`, so the compliance evidence always shows 50000 regardless of how many rows were actually exported
answers.sf11: the Dockerfile copies only detect.py and server.py; the rules/ directory is never COPYed into the image, so RULES_DIR does not exist at runtime, load() returns [], and the detector never matches anything
answers.sf12: the test only asserts status_code in (200,400) and elapsed<5s; both conditions are satisfied whether or not SQL injection is blocked (a 200 with injected results also passes), so the test cannot detect a broken injection defense
answers.sf13: yara is not installed in CI (base extra only), so the test is skipped rather than run; the suite reports 0 failures while the malicious-upload rejection is completely untested
answers.sf16: the cert mount is empty until the CSR is approved, so MTLS_CERT.exists() returns False (or the file exists but is empty/invalid), s.cert is never set, and mTLS is silently not used with no error or log
answers.sf17: helmet is listed under devDependencies and the image runs npm ci --omit=dev, so helmet is null in production, app.use(helmet()) is never called, and all security headers it would set are absent
answers.sf18: audit_stub.go is compiled when the release build tag is set, making Emit a no-op; all audit events are silently dropped in production builds with no log, metric, or error
answers.sf19: the engine globs for *.yaml but the shipped rules files are named threat-*.yml (different extension), so RULES matches nothing, ENGINE is built with zero rules, and detect() never returns True
answers.sf20: every shipped rule declares env: prod but the filter compares r.env == env where env defaults to "production"; "prod" != "production", so active_rules returns [], the engine has zero rules, and the log falsely reports readiness
answers.sf21: the .catch(() => true) swallows any authz error and grants access, silently failing open with no log or metric when the authorization check throws
answers.sf22: contextlib.suppress(Exception) swallows all Redis errors and returns True (allow), so when Redis is unavailable rate limiting is silently disabled with no log, metric, or observable signal
answers.sf23: ProdConfig sets TESTING=True to satisfy a vendor SDK requirement, but BaseConfig derives WTF_CSRF_ENABLED and RATELIMIT_ENABLED as `not TESTING` at class-body evaluation time; both evaluate to False in ProdConfig, silently disabling CSRF protection and rate limiting in production
answers.sf24: requests without Content-Type: application/json return Verdict.ALLOW without inspection; an attacker omits or changes the Content-Type header to bypass WAF scanning entirely
answers.sf25: the X-Forwarded-For / client IP header is attacker-controlled; a remote attacker spoofs a private IP address, IsPrivate() returns True, and DLP inspection is skipped, silently treating hostile traffic as internal
answers.sf26: find_secrets inspects only line[:1024]; a secret placed after byte 1024 in a log line is never examined, so the alert never fires and the secret passes through undetected
answers.sf27: CHAR(32) can only store 32 characters; a SHA-256 hex digest is 64 characters; in non-strict MySQL the value is silently truncated to 32 chars on INSERT, so stored hashes are corrupted and key verification will always fail (or match incorrectly)
answers.sf28: the env var is named ENFORCE_MTLS in deployment.yaml but the code reads MTLS_ENFORCE; the env var is never seen by the server, ENFORCE stays False, and mTLS client verification is silently disabled
answers.sf29: values.yaml sets networkPolicy.enabled but the template checks .Values.networkPolicies.enabled (plural); the key name mismatch means the condition is always false, the NetworkPolicy resource is never created, and network isolation is silently absent
answers.sf30: README says rate limiting is on by default, but install() only adds the middleware when cfg["rate_limit"]["enabled"] is explicitly true; with no rate_limit key in config the middleware is never installed, silently leaving rate limiting off
answers.sf31: docs say telemetry is opt-in and disabled by default, but the Settings dataclass defaults telemetry_enabled=True; telemetry is on by default in every deployment that does not explicitly override it
answers.sf32: the print and sys.exit(0) are unconditional and hardcode "0 findings"; the actual contents of `issues` are never checked, so the security gate always reports PASSED and exits 0 regardless of what the linters found
answers.sf33: include_package_data=False means MANIFEST.in is ignored and policies/*.rego files are not included in the installed package; POLICY_DIR.glob("*.rego") yields nothing, load() returns [], and authorization runs with zero policies
answers.sf34: the embed directive is `//go:embed rules/*.yml` but the repository stores rules under detection/rules/, not rules/ relative to the source file; the embed matches nothing, rulesFS is empty, and Load() returns nil
answers.sf35: mock_authz.assert_called_once is a property access, not a method call; it always evaluates to a bound method object (truthy) without actually asserting anything, so the test passes even if mock_authz was never called
answers.sf36: the entire assertion is inside except Exception: pass; if the request raises or the assertion fails an exception, it is swallowed and the test passes regardless, making it impossible for this test to fail
answers.sf37: expect(res).rejects.toThrow() is not awaited; the assertion returns a Promise that is never settled, the test body completes synchronously and passes unconditionally, so a non-throwing verifyToken would not be caught
answers.sf38: BLOCKED uses re.match which anchors at the start and checks for uppercase keywords, but the docstring says queries arrive lowercased; "select", "union", "drop" never match the pattern, so the block list is always bypassed
answers.sf39: is_authorized is an async function but is called without await; the condition evaluates the coroutine object itself (always truthy), so the 403 branch is never taken and every request is authorized regardless of actual permissions
answers.sf41: cfg is loaded from config.yaml (session_cookie_secure: true) and then cfg.update() overwrites it with defaults.yaml (session_cookie_secure: false); the operator's secure setting is silently overridden by the vendor default
answers.sf42: the context with the 3-second timeout is created but http.NewRequest is called without it (uses context.Background() implicitly via plain http.NewRequest); the timeout context is never attached to the request and has no effect
answers.sf43: after all 3 quarantine attempts fail the loop exits silently and returns {"status": "processed"}, indicating success to the caller while the upload was never quarantined
answers.sf50: the module-level `from .security import validate_token` is immediately shadowed by `def validate_token(tok): return True` defined later in the same module; the middleware calls the local no-op that always returns True, silently bypassing all token validation
answers.sf51: compare_digest is called with EXPECTED compared against itself (EXPECTED, EXPECTED) instead of against received; the comparison is always True, so any or no signature is accepted and the HMAC check never rejects a request
answers.sf52: the for loop contains `return p.allows(ctx)` on the first iteration, so only the first policy is ever evaluated and the function returns immediately; remaining policies are never checked, and if the list is empty it returns True (allow)
answers.sf53: requireAuth is registered after the route handlers; Express middleware executes in registration order, so all /api requests are handled and responses sent before requireAuth runs, meaning authentication is never enforced
answers.sf54: meter.consume raises RuntimeError on overage but the except clause only catches QuotaExceeded; RuntimeError propagates uncaught, the 429 is never returned, and quota enforcement silently fails (either crashing or being swallowed higher up)
answers.sf55: UserSchema(**payload) constructs and validates a schema object but the result is discarded; the raw unvalidated payload is written directly to the database, so schema validation has no effect
answers.sf57: lru_cache caches the result keyed only on org_id, ignoring current_user.id; a second user querying the same org_id receives the cached result from the first user, leaking cross-user authorization data
answers.sf58: signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _drain) is commented out; _drain is never registered as a signal handler, so SIGTERM terminates the process immediately without draining the queue
answers.sf59: bool(os.getenv("STRICT_MODE", "false")) evaluates the string "false" as truthy (non-empty string), so STRICT_MODE is always True regardless of the env var value, making the toggle meaningless
answers.sf60: the autouse fixture patches app.limits.check to always return True for every test in the session without restriction; any test that is supposed to verify rate-limiting behavior will pass even if rate limiting is broken
answers.sf61: os.umask(0o022) is set before write_text, so the file is created with permissions 0o644 (world-readable); the subsequent os.chmod(path, 0o600 | 0o044) sets 0o644 again, so the secret file remains world-readable
answers.sf62: the HTTP request is made before the hostname allowlist check; the response from an arbitrary URL is already fetched (and any SSRF side-effect has occurred) before the check can abort the request
answers.sf63: the security logger's level is set to CRITICAL, so WARNING-level calls to on_authz_denied are silently dropped; authorization denials are never logged
answers.sf64: redis.setex takes the TTL in seconds but SESSION_TTL is 15 (intended as minutes); the session expires after 15 seconds instead of 15 minutes, effectively making sessions unusable rather than enforcing the intended timeout
answers.sf65: IsInternal returns !ip.IsPrivate(), meaning it returns True for public IPs and False for private ones — the logic is inverted; external/public IPs are treated as internal and granted admin route access
answers.sf66: redact(event) returns a new dict with the password masked but the return value is discarded; the original event dict is unchanged, and json.dumps(event) serializes the unredacted original
answers.sf67: the engine uses first-match-wins and the allow *.* rule is listed first; every request matches the allow rule immediately and the deny rule for billing:refund is never reached, so refunds are never blocked
answers.sf68: ENFORCE is evaluated once at module import time from a feature flag; if the flag is later enabled (or the module is imported before the flag service is ready), ENFORCE is permanently False for the process lifetime and MFA is never checked
answers.sf69: when any rule raises an exception it is caught and the loop continues, but if all remaining rules also raise (or there are no more rules), the function returns Verdict.ALLOW; rule failures silently degrade to allow with no metric or alert, only a list that may go unexamined

answers.sf01: model_dir populated by init container may be absent or empty at boot, leaving self.enabled=False and scan() returning [] (allow-all) with no observable error
answers.sf02: clamd is listed under optional dev dependencies and omitted from the production Docker install, so clamd is always None in prod and scan_upload always returns {"clean": True}
answers.sf03: the shipped example config contains only commented-out rules, so rules=[] and Waf.blocks() always returns False — WAF appears enabled (logged) but matches nothing
answers.sf04: policy engine error causes fail-open (returns true/allow) instead of deny, making authorization silently ineffective whenever the engine is unavailable
answers.sf05: when DEBUG env var is set to '1', verify_signature is skipped entirely, allowing unauthenticated webhook processing with no error or observable indication
answers.sf06: bodies larger than MAX_INSPECT (1 MiB) are silently allowed without inspection, so an attacker can bypass DLP by sending a payload just over the size threshold
answers.sf07: only the first 8192 bytes are scanned but the full payload is stored, so malicious content appended after the first 8 KB passes the scanner and is persisted
answers.sf08: the enforce field is parsed into ScannerCfg but logging.enforce is not a field in LoggingCfg, so the operator-supplied value is silently ignored and enforce stays False
answers.sf09: the default value for verify_signatures is False while docs claim the default is true, so signature verification is disabled unless explicitly set, with no warning
answers.sf10: the completion message hardcodes '50000 events written' regardless of how many rows were actually written, so operators may certify an incomplete export as complete
answers.sf11: the Dockerfile copies only detect.py and server.py but not the rules/ directory, so RULES_DIR never exists at runtime and load() always returns [], leaving detection permanently disabled
answers.sf12: the test only checks status code and elapsed time, not whether the query was actually parameterized or safe; a timing-based or error-suppressing injection would pass
answers.sf13: yara is not installed in CI (only base extras), so the test is always skipped and the malicious-upload rejection is never verified, reporting 0 failures silently
answers.sf16: the mTLS cert mount is empty until CSR approval, so MTLS_CERT.exists() is False and s.cert is never set, meaning mTLS is silently disabled with no error or warning
answers.sf17: helmet is listed under devDependencies and omitted when the production image runs npm ci --omit=dev, so helmet is always null in prod and no security headers are set
answers.sf18: the release build tag causes audit.Emit to be a no-op, so all audit events are silently dropped in production builds with no error or indication
answers.sf19: the rules package ships files named threat-*.yml but the glob pattern is *.yaml (different extension), so RULES is always empty and ENGINE never matches anything
answers.sf20: all shipped rules declare env: prod but the filter compares against APP_ENV defaulting to 'production', so env=='prod' never equals 'production' and active_rules returns []
answers.sf21: the .catch(() => true) makes any authz error fail open, so an exception from the policy engine silently grants permission to delete
answers.sf22: any Redis exception causes the function to return True (allow), so rate limiting is silently bypassed whenever Redis is unavailable
answers.sf23: ProdConfig sets TESTING=True, which causes WTF_CSRF_ENABLED and RATELIMIT_ENABLED to evaluate to False (not True), disabling CSRF and rate limiting in production
answers.sf24: requests without Content-Type: application/json are unconditionally allowed without WAF inspection, so an attacker bypasses the WAF by omitting or changing the content-type header
answers.sf25: IsInternal returns !ip.IsPrivate(), meaning private/internal IPs are considered 'not internal' and DLP inspection is applied to them, while public IPs are treated as internal and bypass inspection — the logic is inverted
answers.sf26: find_secrets truncates lines to 1024 bytes before regex matching, so secrets positioned after byte 1024 in a log line are never detected
answers.sf27: SHA-256 hex is 64 characters but the column is CHAR(32), so in non-strict MySQL the hash is silently truncated to 32 chars on insert, storing a partial hash that cannot correctly verify keys
answers.sf28: the env var is named ENFORCE_MTLS in deployment.yaml but the code reads MTLS_ENFORCE, so ENFORCE is always False and mTLS is never required despite the deployment config
answers.sf29: the Helm values key is networkPolicy.enabled but the template checks .Values.networkPolicies.enabled (plural), so the NetworkPolicy is never rendered regardless of the value set
answers.sf30: the README states rate limiting is enabled by default, but the code only activates it when cfg['rate_limit']['enabled'] is explicitly truthy; with no rate_limit key in config it is silently disabled
answers.sf31: docs state telemetry is opt-in and disabled by default, but the code defaults telemetry_enabled to True, so telemetry is always on unless explicitly disabled
answers.sf32: the script unconditionally prints 'PASSED — 0 findings' and exits 0 regardless of what run_linters() returned, so real findings are silently suppressed
answers.sf33: include_package_data=False and MANIFEST.in is ignored, so policies/*.rego files are not included in the installed package; POLICY_DIR.is_dir() returns False and load() returns []
answers.sf34: the embed directive references rules/*.yml but the repository stores rules in detection/rules/, so the embedded FS is empty and Load() always returns nil
answers.sf35: mock_authz.assert_called_once is a property access, not a method call; it never raises and the assertion always passes even if the mock was never called
answers.sf36: the bare except swallows all exceptions including assertion errors, so a traversal that causes an exception (or returns the wrong status) silently passes the test
answers.sf37: verifyToken returns a Promise but the test does not await res or use return, so the expect().rejects chain is never awaited and the test always passes regardless of behavior
answers.sf38: the regex uses re.match which anchors at the start but queries arrive already lowercased while the pattern matches only uppercase keywords, so BLOCKED never matches and all queries pass
answers.sf39: is_authorized is an async function but is called without await, so the check always receives a truthy coroutine object and the authorization check never actually executes
answers.sf40: the @require_admin decorator is applied before @app.route, so Flask registers the already-decorated (wrapped) function; depending on decorator order this may work, but if require_admin is not route-aware the auth check may be bypassed — however this is framework/decorator-implementation dependent; NONE
answers.sf41: cfg.update(defaults) overwrites the already-loaded config with defaults, so session_cookie_secure ends up as False (from defaults.yaml) instead of True (from config.yaml)
answers.sf42: the context timeout is set but the http.Request is created without the context (http.NewRequest instead of http.NewRequestWithContext), so the timeout is never attached to the request
answers.sf43: if all three quarantine attempts fail, the loop exits silently and the function returns {"status": "processed"}, indicating success even though quarantine never succeeded
answers.sf50: the local definition of validate_token (returning True) shadows the imported one from .security, so the middleware always passes authentication regardless of the token
answers.sf51: hmac.compare_digest compares EXPECTED against itself (EXPECTED, EXPECTED) instead of against received, so the signature check always succeeds and any signature is accepted
answers.sf52: the for loop immediately returns the result of the first policy's allows() on the first iteration, so only one policy is ever evaluated and the rest are silently ignored
answers.sf53: requireAuth is registered after the routes in Express middleware order, so it never runs for any /api route and all API endpoints are unauthenticated
answers.sf54: meter.consume raises RuntimeError on overage but the except clause only catches QuotaExceeded, so RuntimeError propagates uncaught and quota enforcement silently fails
answers.sf55: UserSchema(**payload) validates the payload but the result is discarded; the raw unvalidated payload is written directly to the database, making the validation a no-op
answers.sf57: lru_cache caches results keyed only on org_id (current_user.id is captured from the outer scope at call time but is not a cache key parameter), so one user's authorized projects may be returned to a different user
answers.sf58: the signal.signal call is commented out, so _drain is never registered as a SIGTERM handler and in-flight queue items are not drained on shutdown
answers.sf59: bool(os.getenv('STRICT_MODE', 'false')) is always True because any non-empty string including 'false' is truthy, so STRICT_MODE is always True regardless of intent — but this means the check is always enabled, not silently disabled; NONE
answers.sf60: the autouse=True fixture with no marker restriction patches out rate limiting for all tests including integration/security tests, so rate-limit enforcement is never tested
answers.sf61: os.umask(0o022) is called before write_text, which creates the file with mode 0o644 (world-readable), and the subsequent chmod to 0o600|0o044=0o644 does not restrict it; the secret is world-readable during the window between creation and chmod (TOCTOU), and the final chmod sets 0o644 not 0o600
answers.sf62: the SSRF hostname check is performed after the HTTP request has already been made, so the request to a forbidden host completes before the check can block it
answers.sf63: the security logger's level is set to CRITICAL but on_authz_denied logs at WARNING level, which is below CRITICAL, so all authorization denial events are silently dropped
answers.sf64: setex takes TTL in seconds but SESSION_TTL is 15 (intended as minutes), so sessions expire after 15 seconds instead of 15 minutes
answers.sf65: IsInternal returns !ip.IsPrivate(), which is true for public IPs, so admin routes are granted to external/public IPs and blocked for internal ones — the logic is inverted
answers.sf66: redact(event) returns a new dict with the password masked but the return value is discarded; the original unredacted event dict is serialized and written to the sink
answers.sf67: the engine uses first-match-wins, so the allow */* rule matches everything before the deny billing:refund rule is ever reached, making the deny rule permanently unreachable
answers.sf68: ENFORCE is evaluated at module import time from a feature flag; if the flag is toggled on after import, ENFORCE remains False for the lifetime of the process and MFA is never enforced
answers.sf69: when a rule raises an exception it is silently appended to failed and the loop continues; after all rules are processed the function returns ALLOW, so rule errors cause fail-open behavior with no blocking
